contentBlocks.original:
लोकाभिरामं रणरङ्गधीरं राजीवनेत्रं रघुवंशनाथम् ।
कारुण्यरुपं करुणाकरंतं श्रीरामचंद्रं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lokābhirāmaṃ raṇaraṅgadhīraṃ rājīvanetraṃ raghuvaṃśanātham .
kāruṇyarupaṃ karuṇākarantaṃ śrīrāmacandraṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadye ..
contentBlocks.translation: I take refuge in Sri Ramachandra, who is beloved of all people, who is steadfast in battle, whose eyes are like lotuses, who is the lord of the Raghu dynasty, who is the embodiment of compassion, and who showers mercy.
contentBlocks.meanings: लोकाभिरामं (lokābhirāmam) – pleasing to the people (loka:people | abhirāmam:pleasing); रणरङ्गधीरं (raṇaraṅgadhīram) – calm in the battlefield (raṇa:battle | raṅga:field | dhīram:calm); राजीवनेत्रं (rājīvanetram) – lotus-eyed (rājīva:lotus | netram:eye); रघुवंशनाथम् (raghuvaṃśanātham) – lord of Raghu dynasty (raghu:Raghu | vaṃśa:dynasty | nātham:lord); कारुण्यरुपं (kāruṇyarupam) – embodiment of compassion (kāruṇya:compassion | rupam:form); करुणाकरंतं (karuṇākarantaṃ) – showering compassion (karuṇa:compassion | karantam:showering); श्रीरामचंद्रं (śrīrāmacandraṃ) – Sri Ramachandra (śrī:Sri | rāma:Rama | candraṃ:Chandra); शरणं (śaraṇaṃ) – refuge; प्रपद्ये (prapadye) – I take refuge;
